_schema_note: 1.1 = test_results split into backend/frontend sub-objects now that frontend unit tests exist (EM-043); everything else mirrors the W9 1.0 shape.

resolution.summary: Wave-2 QA scope was EM-043: stand up frontend unit-test infrastructure and regression-proof the four W9-audit frontend bugs (C2 play/pause ref-in-render, C4 strict-left fold boundary, C5 cleanup-captured ref, C6 gov-column attribution) plus the W10 wave-1 selector additions. Delivered: vitest 3.2.6 + jsdom + @testing-library in web/ (standalone vitest.config.ts — the production vite build pipeline is untouched), `npm test` / `npm run test:watch` scripts, and 63 tests across 11 files: 49 pure-logic tests (replayStateAt incl. the C7 building projection, agentEconomyAt, governanceTimeline + awiSummary, computeRoutingHealthSmoothed + the useRoutingHealth recovered transient, extinction lib, animalIdentity) and 14 component smokes (ReplayScrubber, AgentPanels, ExtinctionBanner, EventFeed, SocialGraph). C2, C4, C6 and W9-QA-1 are now natively pinned. The C5 test FOUND A NEW BUG (W10-QA-1, see issues): the shipped C5 fix is provably dead code — pinned with a strict `it.fails` xfail (the project's pytest-xfail convention) that flips loudly when fixed. All three validation gates re-run green.

contract_conformance.notes: The frontend selectors now have native pins on every contract point the last two waves' bugs touched: snapshot-at-S = post-tick-S state with a STRICT-LEFT fold (event-log.md v1.1.0 §3 / audit C4 — event AT base.tick excluded, base.tick+1 included, no-snapshot base of -1 applies tick-0 events); agent_moved destination read place → to → location → target_id with place winning over a disagreeing `to` (W9-QA-1, pinned at BOTH consumer sites: the replay fold and the awiSummary space-exploration cut); C7 building state at T is a pure event fold where the live roster never supplies status (only display metadata), born-after-T live buildings are dropped, and pre-window buildings rewind through structure_state_changed.from; C6 governance pass/reject attribution goes to the PROPOSER's model even when the resolution event carries a different profile; agentEconomyAt anchors on turn_start samples and folds only the agent's own state_deltas (EM-075). The W10-QA-1 finding is a dead app-level safety net, not a contract violation — the rAF/battery behavior the contract cares about is delivered by force-graph's own _destructor.

test_coverage.notes: Frontend: 63 tests / 11 files, first frontend suite in the repo (EM-043 closes). Pure-logic core (49): selectors.replay 15, selectors.governance+AWI 6, selectors.economy 5, routing-health hysteresis 10 (incl. the exact implementer smoke scenarios: 4-don't-trip/5th-trips, 1-diverse-doesn't-clear/2nd-clears, fresh-full-run-to-re-show, animal exclusion, single-profile immunity, and the transient `recovered` flag via renderHook + fake timers), extinction 6, animalIdentity 7. Component smokes (14): ReplayScrubber play/pause render state (C2) + play-advances-tick + speed aria-pressed, AgentPanels dying badge presence/absence/singular, ExtinctionBanner alert/CTA/fallback/hidden, EventFeed starving-warn + extinct-danger registers, SocialGraph mount + the W10-QA-1 strict xfail pin. Gaps, documented not dodged: useSimulation's synthetic-seq scheme (audit C10) and reconnect/backoff (C3) are closures inside the hook — mounting it opens a real WebSocket + paged /api/events backfill, so unit-testing them needs either a pure extraction or heavy network mocking; verified by source inspection only (useSimulation.ts:102-103, 464-466). Backend suite unchanged this wave (re-run only).

security.notes: This wave-2 adds devDependencies and test files only — no new runtime surface, no production-bundle change (vite build output verified; vitest config is standalone and not imported by the build). W10 wave 1 already landed the deferred audit items B11 (Gemini key via x-goog-api-key header) and B15 (spawn field length caps) per the wave-1 gate log, and the re-run backend suite (188 green) covers them. Local-lab posture (no auth on god endpoints) unchanged and accepted by design.

regression_risk.notes: Zero non-test source files were modified (ownership honored; the W10-QA-1 bug found by the C5 test was documented + xfail-pinned instead of fixed). Validations all green after the test infra landed: vitest 63/63; `npx tsc -b` clean (test files are type-checked under the strict app tsconfig); `npm run build` clean (pre-existing >500kB chunk warning only); eslint clean on all new files; backend `python -m pytest -q` 188 passed / 0 failed (the W9 strict xfail was flipped to a passing assertion by wave 1 and stays green). The new tests are deterministic: fake timers for the play loop + recovered transient, no network, no real canvas (jsdom canvas stubbed to null, which every component under test already guards).

backend.notes: Re-run only (no backend edits this wave-2). W9's strict xfail (W9-QA-1b, repository.py space_exploration) was fixed and flipped in W10 wave 1 — the suite now passes it as a real assertion.

frontend.infra: vitest ^3.2.6 + jsdom ^26.1.0 + @testing-library/react ^16.3.2 + @testing-library/jest-dom ^6.9.1 + @testing-library/user-event ^14.6.1 (devDeps); web/vitest.config.ts (jsdom, globals, src/**/*.test.*, setup stubs ResizeObserver + canvas getContext); scripts: test / test:watch
frontend.xfail_note: The 1 xfail pin uses vitest `it.fails` (strict: it turns RED the moment the underlying W10-QA-1 bug is fixed, exactly like the project's pytest strict-xfail convention).

test_results.frontend_verification: cd web && npx tsc -b — clean (test files included under the strict app tsconfig); npm run build — clean (pre-existing >500kB chunk-size warning only, bundle unchanged by the test infra); npx eslint on all new test files — clean

issues.title: W10-QA-1: the audit-C5 fix in SocialGraph is dead code — the cleanup reads a ref React has already detached (found by the new unit test, xfail-pinned, NOT fixed per ownership rules)
issues.detail: web/src/inspector/SocialGraph.tsx:136-143 pauses the force-graph on unmount via `fgRef.current?.pauseAnimation()` inside a useEffect cleanup, with a comment asserting 'reading it at teardown IS the fix'. Empirically false on react@18.3.1: React detaches forwarded refs (useImperativeHandle sets ref.current = null) during the commit mutation phase, BEFORE passive effect cleanups run — a scratch harness confirmed a parent cleanup sees null, and react-force-graph-2d forwards its handle via the same useImperativeHandle mechanism (react-kapsule). So pauseAnimation is never called from the component. SEVERITY IS LOW because the battery goal still holds through a different path: react-kapsule invokes the kapsule `_destructor` on unmount, and force-graph's _destructor calls this.pauseAnimation() itself (node_modules/force-graph/dist/force-graph.mjs:1354) — the rAF loop does stop. The defect is false confidence: an app-level safety net that provably never fires, plus a source comment claiming the opposite. Pinned by src/inspector/SocialGraph.test.tsx with a strict `it.fails` xfail that flips RED when someone makes the pause actually fire (e.g. capture the instance via the ref-callback/onEngineStop path, or drop the effect and rely explicitly on the lib destructor with a comment). File into the ledger via plan-intake; suggested wave: W11 hygiene.

issues.title: useSimulation internals (audit C10 synthetic-seq, C3 reconnect/backoff) remain unit-untested — not cleanly extractable without src changes
issues.detail: The collision-free synthetic-seq counter (negative decrementing; useSimulation.ts:102-103, used at :464-466) and the reconnect logic are closures inside the hook; mounting it opens a real WebSocket plus a paged /api/events backfill fetch loop, so a unit test would need to mock the network world — explicitly discouraged by the EM-043 brief ('prefer extracting testable pieces over mocking the world'), and extraction is a src change outside qe ownership. Verified by source inspection only this wave: the counter starts at -1 and only decrements, so it cannot collide with positive server/mock seqs. If a future wave touches useSimulation.ts, extract `nextSyntheticSeq` (and ideally the merge/dedupe reducers at :109-150, which are pure) into a lib module and add the trivial tests then.

gate_decision.reason: EM-043 is delivered: the repo's first frontend unit-test suite (63 tests, 11 files) is green and natively regression-pins all four named W9-audit bugs (C2 play/pause render state, C4 strict-left fold boundary, C6 proposer-model gov attribution, and C5 — whose test immediately earned its keep by exposing W10-QA-1) plus W9-QA-1's place-first chain at both consumer sites and the W10 wave-1 selector additions (C7 building projection, EM-075 economy re-projection, EM-072 hysteresis). All three validation gates pass: vitest 63/63, tsc -b + vite build clean (production pipeline untouched by the test infra), backend 188/188 re-confirmed. The one new finding (W10-QA-1) is LOW severity — the library's own destructor delivers the pause the dead app-level cleanup was meant to guarantee — and is strict-xfail-pinned so it cannot rot silently. No blockers: GREEN to proceed to gate 3 (orchestrator live verification), with W10-QA-1 and the useSimulation-extraction note carried as tracked follow-ups.
